subject: GRB 200308A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 22:35:15 UT on 8 Mar 2020, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 200308A (trigger 605399720.791064 / 200308941).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 327.4, Dec = -27.8 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 21h 49m, -27d 48'), with a statistical uncertainty of 11.9 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 64.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200308941/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn200308941.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200308941/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn200308941.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200308941/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn200308941.gif
